title: SecureJoin
description: Protect your community with server-wide or per-user passwords. Keep unwanted players out while letting trusted users in easily.
body:
# SecureJoin
SecureJoin adds a security layer to your Minecraft server by requiring players to enter a password before fully joining. Perfect for small communities, or private servers. SecureJoin helps prevent account hijacking and unauthorized access.

Utilizes Minecraft 1.21.6's dialogs for a better UI/UX design.

## Features
- Server-wide password protection
- Per-user account passwords
- IP-based session remembering ("Remember this device")
- Lockout system after too many failed attempts (optional)
- Server isolation before authentication
- Admin tools to reset user passwords

## Commands
- `/setserverpassword` - Opens a dialog to set the server's password, and configure lockout options.
- `/setuserpassword` - Opens a dialog to set your own account password
- `/resetuserpassword <player>` - Resets the player's password, and requires them to set a new one on the next login.
